title: Korean Kimchi Jjigae (Tofu Kimchi Stew)

subs.name: 200 g shredded cabbage sautéed with 1 tbsp gochujang and 1 tsp vinegar for 5 minutes as a quick stewing substitute
200 g paneer, cubed — holds its shape better; add in the last 5 minutes to prevent crumbling
100 g spinach leaves, added in the last 2 minutes only — they wilt faster than mushroom

steps.text: Cut the tofu into 2 cm cubes and press gently between layers of kitchen paper for a few minutes to remove excess moisture so the cubes hold their shape during simmering.

Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high flame. Add minced garlic and grated ginger, sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the kimchi and stir-fry for 3 minutes until it darkens slightly and the sour smell mellows into a deeper, richer aroma.
Stir in gochujang and soy sauce, then pour in 600 ml water. Bring to a rolling boil, scraping any caramelised bits from the base of the pot.
Add tofu cubes and sliced mushrooms. Reduce heat to a steady simmer, cover the pot and cook for 15 minutes until the broth is deeply flavoured, brick-red and slightly reduced.
Taste for salt — kimchi is already salty so go carefully. Add the spring onion whites and simmer uncovered for 2 more minutes. Turn off the heat.
Drizzle gingelly oil over the top, scatter sesame seeds and spring onion greens. Carry the pot directly to the table and serve with steamed rice on the side — the stew stays hot in the pot.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tofu → 200 g paneer, cubed — holds its shape better; add in the last 5 minutes to prevent crumbling; kimchi → 200 g shredded cabbage sautéed with 1 tbsp gochujang and 1 tsp vinegar for 5 minutes as a quick stewing substitute; mushroom → 100 g spinach leaves, added in the last 2 minutes only — they wilt faster than mushroom (optional variation)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Kimchi jjigae cooks beautifully in a heavy-bottomed pot or deep kadai, much like a dal. Use vegetarian kimchi (labelled thus in Korean stores; it contains no fish sauce) to keep this suitable for a vegetarian kitchen. Firm tofu is sold as 'firm tofu' or 'bean curd' in most Indian supermarkets in cities; a block of paneer can substitute in a pinch — add it in the last 5 minutes since it softens faster.
